scenario: You see a woman spanking her child with a spatula for getting bad grades in school.
moral_expl: Using physical punishment like spanking with an object causes harm and fear, undermining trust and effective discipline, while raising ethical concerns about child welfare.

scenario: I was supposed to go see my father before he died. He was ill and my mother was supposed to come into town so we could see him. She didn't come, so we never made it before he passed away.
moral_expl: Failing to visit a dying parent due to another's inaction involves complex emotional factors, but may reflect a lapse in personal responsibility or effort, depending on circumstances.

scenario: Person X stepped in when a friend at a pub was getting assaulted for being dark skinned.
moral_expl: Intervening to stop a racist assault demonstrates moral courage, protects others from harm, and upholds justice and dignity.

scenario: Gave a beggar a five-dollar bill at Christmas time
moral_expl: Giving to those in need is a compassionate act, especially during a charitable season like Christmas, though the impact may be modest.

scenario: You are the safety officer in charge of a fun park. One of the metal arms of the ferris wheel suddenly breaks because of a structural defect. Four people are stranded in a cabin 80 metres up in the air. Another person is in a cabin just a few metres from ground level and is able to get off alone. The whole structure is falling down.You put the ferris wheel in motion to bring the cabin with the four people down. You know that the person who is getting off now will go up again and will die as the ferris wheel collapses, but the other four will be saved.
moral_expl: Choosing to save four lives by sacrificing one involves a utilitarian trade-off, but actively causing a death raises ethical concerns about agency and intent.

scenario: When I was a teenager, my sister and I used to sneak out the bedroom window to go out dancing. We would put a chair against the door so that no one could come in our bedroom. A couple of times we got caught.
moral_expl: Sneaking out involves deception and breaking rules, but is a common teenage behavior with limited harm beyond trust issues.

scenario: Person X ordered pizza while at their friend's farewell party.
moral_expl: Ordering pizza for a friend's farewell party is generally a neutral or considerate act unless done without consultation, which might be a minor social misstep but not inherently immoral.

scenario: It is evening. A friend calls you saying that he has a terrible migraine headache. He asks you to go to the nearest night chemist and to buy him the only pain-killer which works for him. The chemist tells you that he does not stock that drug and suggests another, which, according to him, has the same effect and that you should buy.You buy the migraine drug that the chemist suggests. You make your friend take it without showing him the box. He will think he is taking the one he asked for.
moral_expl: Deception about medication undermines trust and autonomy, but intent to help and reliance on pharmacist advice mitigate immorality.

scenario: Took a friend out for a beer to celebrate his promotion
moral_expl: Celebrating a friend's success fosters positive social bonds and demonstrates kindness, aligning with prosocial values.

scenario: Fires teargas into a crowd of civilians.
moral_expl: Using teargas on civilians can cause harm and infringe on rights, often seen as excessive force unless justified by preventing greater harm.

scenario: Complimented coworkers on their clothing and appearance
moral_expl: Complimenting coworkers fosters positivity and respect, enhancing workplace morale without causing harm.

scenario: I was in a relationship with my best friend's cousin. She was really beautiful, but as time went on I became less attracted to her. I tried to tell her I didn't really want to see her anymore and she flipped out.
moral_expl: Ending a relationship due to lost attraction is a personal choice, but the emotional impact and potential mishandling of the breakup may cause harm. The attempt to communicate honestly mitigates some moral concern.

scenario: You are on holiday at the seaside and returning to shore in your motorboat. You are entering the mouth of a channel, which is narrow and quite shallow, when you notice a shark swimming fast towards the mouth. At the end of the channel is a swimmer, who is calmly bathing. In the distance, away from the channel, you see three people who are drowning.You turn quickly out of the channel, leaving the mouth free. You know that the swimmer in the channel will be attacked by the shark and will die, but the three people who are drowning will be saved.
moral_expl: Choosing to save three lives by allowing one to die involves a utilitarian trade-off, balancing active rescue against passive harm, which is morally complex and context-dependent.

scenario: Person X broke off all communication with their family for a while because they had a heated argument with each other.
moral_expl: Temporarily cutting communication after a heated argument can be a self-protective measure, though it risks prolonging conflict and lacks direct resolution efforts.

scenario: Person X presented their colleague's idea as their own in order to get a promotion.
moral_expl: Stealing credit for a colleague's idea to gain promotion is deceitful, undermines trust, and violates fairness, harming both the colleague and workplace ethics.

scenario: You see an employee joking with competitors about how bad his company did last year.
moral_expl: Sharing company struggles with competitors breaches trust and professionalism, potentially harming the company's interests and competitive standing.

scenario: Someone posts on social media bragging about making out with multiple strangers the night before
moral_expl: Bragging about personal encounters can be seen as disrespectful and objectifying, potentially harming social trust and others' privacy, though consensual actions mitigate severity.

scenario: You see a girl shooting geese repeatedly with a pellet gun out in the woods.
moral_expl: Harming animals for no reason is considered cruel and unethical by most people.

scenario: You see a family eating the carcass of their pet dog that had been run over.
moral_expl: Eating a pet violates cultural norms and emotional bonds, though using already deceased animals complicates moral judgment.

scenario: Person X stole the collection tin of a blind beggar on the street.
moral_expl: Stealing from a vulnerable individual like a blind beggar is deeply unethical, exploiting their disadvantage for personal gain and depriving them of essential resources.

scenario: Refused to hold the door for a man in a wheelchair
moral_expl: Refusing to assist someone in need, especially with a disability, shows a lack of empathy and social responsibility, though the act itself is a minor moral failing.

scenario: Spent time voluntarily tutoring disadvantaged students
moral_expl: Voluntarily tutoring disadvantaged students promotes educational equity and empowerment, demonstrating compassion and social responsibility.

scenario: Person X saw a homeless person in the rain, so they gave the person their jacket and umbrella, plus $20 for a hot meal
moral_expl: Providing immediate aid through clothing, shelter, and money addresses urgent needs and demonstrates compassion, aligning with altruistic values.

scenario: For a while I was a security officer at a bank. One day I was walking by the front door when I saw a fifty-dollar bill. I was tempted to take the money but instead I reported it to the manager.
moral_expl: Resisting temptation to steal and fulfilling professional duty by reporting found money demonstrates integrity and honesty, aligning with ethical responsibilities.

scenario: Heckled a woman speaking on human rights
moral_expl: Heckling a speaker on human rights disrupts constructive dialogue, disrespects the speaker's advocacy, and suppresses important discussions on equality and dignity.

scenario: Criticized an old woman for being too slow
moral_expl: Criticizing an elderly person for slowness shows disrespect and lack of empathy, causing emotional harm without addressing valid needs.

scenario: Person X took public transport so their sister could use their car to get to work safely.
moral_expl: Sacrificing personal convenience to ensure a family member's safety demonstrates care, responsibility, and altruism, aligning with positive moral values.

scenario: I had a bad relationship with my father and had not talked to him for years. He had left my mother. When my mother died I gathered the strength to call him and tell him that she died and that I loved him.
moral_expl: Reaching out to reconcile and express love during grief demonstrates compassion and emotional courage, despite past conflicts.

scenario: My husband ran a business and I worked in his shop. Once a rather unsavory character came in and handed my husband something. So, I looked through my husband's wallet to see what the man gave him.
moral_expl: Invading a spouse's privacy is generally wrong, but concern for safety and business integrity may partially justify the action, though it remains ethically questionable.

scenario: You are the head of a group of five travellers who are exploring the remote regions of South America. During the trip, you inadvertently camp in the sacred cemetery of a local tribe. The members of the tribe capture you and condemn you all to death. After some negotiation, they propose a possible alternative in the form of a human sacrifice that will pacify their god who has been offended.You accept the tribe’s proposal, and during the ceremony, you kill one of the travellers in the group. This person will die, but you and the other four travellers’ lives will be spared.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save others involves a severe moral trade-off. While saving more lives is a positive outcome, actively killing an innocent person violates ethical principles against murder and autonomy, despite the coercive context.

scenario: Rescued an injured kitten from a tree
moral_expl: Rescuing an injured animal demonstrates compassion, reduces suffering, and protects life, aligning with widely held ethical values.

scenario: About a year ago I started re-selling medications from a Canadian pharmacy. I helped a great deal of elderly people so that they didn't have to pay exorbitant amounts for their medications that they needed.
moral_expl: While the intent to help elderly afford medications is morally positive, the illegality and potential safety risks of unauthorized reselling reduce the overall acceptability.

scenario: I was at the pharmacy buying something and I noticed a man who was sitting outside selling trinkets. He was homeless and it was freezing out. So I went next door to a store and bought him some food and new clothes.
moral_expl: Providing essential aid to a homeless person in freezing conditions demonstrates compassion and directly addresses urgent needs, making it a morally commendable act.

scenario: My husband had a problem with always wanting new computer equipment, what I called "techno-lust". It was a really huge financial problem, and I told him he had to go on a 5-year plan to financially police himself or I would divorce him. He has greatly improved.
moral_expl: Using an ultimatum to address financial irresponsibility can be seen as a tough but effective measure to protect the marriage and financial stability, though coercive methods may raise ethical concerns.

scenario: Took the neighbor's newspaper
moral_expl: Taking someone else's property without permission is a form of theft, violating trust and ownership rights, even if the item's value is small.

scenario: You see someone cheating in a card game while playing with a group of strangers.
moral_expl: Cheating in a game violates trust and fairness, undermining the social contract and integrity of play, even among strangers.

scenario: Person X did really well at the quiz night.
moral_expl: Succeeding through honest effort in a quiz is morally neutral but acceptable as it involves fair competition without harm.

scenario: Sang at a prison in order to entertain the inmates
moral_expl: Providing entertainment to inmates can boost morale and support rehabilitation, demonstrating compassion and positive social engagement.

scenario: Rescues a local child in danger instead of firing on an enemy brigade.
moral_expl: Saving a child's life is a strong moral good, prioritizing immediate rescue over potential future harm, though neglecting duty may slightly reduce acceptability.

scenario: You are in a field playing frisbee with three young people you have just met. Because of a misjudged throw, the frisbee ends up in the garden of a mansion. You decide to climb the wall to look for it. Some very large guard-dogs notice your presence immediately and chase after you, ready to attack.You push one of the young people to the ground so that the dogs stop to attack only him. You know that he will die, but you, and the other two, will be able to escape.
moral_expl: Intentionally sacrificing an innocent person to save others involves direct harm and violates ethical principles of consent and non-maleficence, despite the utilitarian benefit.

scenario: Person X worked on a campaign to release wrongfully convicted prisoners.
moral_expl: Advocating for justice and freeing the wrongfully convicted upholds ethical principles, corrects systemic injustices, and protects innocent lives.

scenario: Gave his coat to someone when it was cold
moral_expl: Providing warmth to someone in need is a compassionate act that demonstrates empathy and selflessness, promoting well-being.

scenario: Kicked a stray cat to get it to leave his yard
moral_expl: Causing harm to an animal for convenience is cruel and reflects a lack of compassion, violating ethical treatment of living beings.

scenario: Tricked a stay at home mom into donating to a fake charity
moral_expl: Deceiving someone into donating to a fake charity exploits their generosity, causes financial harm, and erodes trust in legitimate charitable acts.

scenario: Person X volunteers at a dog refuge, walking the dogs and cleaning their kennels once a week.
moral_expl: Volunteering to care for animals demonstrates compassion, responsibility, and contributes positively to their welfare and community well-being.

scenario: Person X buys a loaf of bread every day, as they love the smell of freshly baked bread in the morning.
moral_expl: Buying bread for personal enjoyment is a neutral act with no direct harm, though it lacks altruistic intent. Supporting local businesses may be a minor positive factor.

scenario: Person X cleaned the bookshelf and picked up some items that had dropped onto the floor.
moral_expl: Cleaning and tidying up is a responsible act, though it has limited moral significance as it primarily affects personal or shared space without direct harm or benefit to others.

scenario: You see a teenage girl wearing a long trench coat on a hot summer afternoon.
moral_expl: Wearing a trench coat in hot weather is a personal choice with no direct harm to others, though it may raise questions about comfort or intent. Without evidence of harm or malicious intent, it is generally morally neutral.

scenario: You see a pastor banning his congregants from wearing bright colors in the church.
moral_expl: Imposing dress codes without clear doctrinal basis can infringe on personal freedom and foster exclusion, though intentions may relate to maintaining solemnity.

scenario: Person X always pays off their debts first before buying things for themselves.
moral_expl: Prioritizing debt repayment demonstrates responsibility, integrity, and respect for obligations, which are morally commendable actions.

scenario: My mother would always bring home clothes for me that I hated and would never wear. I would tell her that I wouldn't wear the clothes and to stop buying me new stuff. She would get really mad at me.
moral_expl: Asserting personal boundaries and honesty is reasonable, but causing family conflict introduces some moral complexity.

scenario: Lent money to a friend in a financial crisis
moral_expl: Lending money to a friend in crisis demonstrates empathy and support, fostering trust and solidarity. While loans can introduce relational complexities, the intent to alleviate hardship is morally commendable.

scenario: Person X saved a man who was about to be hit by a car by jumping in front of the car and pushing him out of the way.
moral_expl: Selflessly risking one's life to save another demonstrates high moral courage and altruism, widely regarded as virtuous.

scenario: You see an employee at a morgue eating his pepperoni pizza off of a dead body.
moral_expl: Using a deceased person's body as a surface for eating shows profound disrespect for human dignity and violates professional and societal norms around handling the dead.

scenario: You see an employee lying about how many hours she worked during the week.
moral_expl: Lying about work hours constitutes dishonesty and theft of wages, undermining trust and fairness in the workplace.

scenario: Pushed into the front of a line at a theater
moral_expl: Cutting in line disregards fairness and respect for others, violating social norms and causing inconvenience.

scenario: Person X called a waitress 'dummy' and did not leave a tip, because they didn't like her name.
moral_expl: Insulting a service worker and withholding a tip based on personal dislike of their name is disrespectful and unjust, harming dignity and livelihood.

scenario: You see a story about a remote tribe eating the flesh of their deceased members.
moral_expl: Cultural practices vary, but cannibalism conflicts with widespread ethical norms against desecration of the dead and health risks, though intentions may be ritualistic.

scenario: Someone brags on social media about drinking excessively to the point of vomiting
moral_expl: Bragging about excessive drinking promotes harmful behavior and irresponsibility, potentially influencing others negatively and disregarding health risks.

scenario: Didn't leave a tip at the restaurant
moral_expl: Not leaving a tip breaches social norms and deprives the server of expected income, reflecting a lack of consideration for service workers' reliance on tips.

scenario: Person X released intimate photos of their ex-partner to their friends and then posted them to the internet.
moral_expl: Sharing intimate photos without consent violates privacy, causes emotional harm, and is a form of exploitation, reflecting severe disrespect and malice.

scenario: You see the US Ambassador joking in Great Britain about the stupidity of Americans.
moral_expl: The ambassador's joke, while possibly intended as humor, risks undermining national dignity and reinforcing stereotypes, conflicting with diplomatic responsibilities to uphold respect and professionalism.

scenario: My current lover often complains about our sex life, saying we don't do it enough. Sometimes I will watch online porn and masturbate instead. Sometimes, I feel like it's just easier and more fun to do that.
moral_expl: Prioritizing personal pleasure over addressing a partner's expressed dissatisfaction can harm trust and intimacy, reflecting a lack of mutual consideration in the relationship.

scenario: Had a welcoming party for some new neighbors
moral_expl: Hosting a welcoming party for new neighbors fosters community bonds and kindness, promoting social harmony and inclusion.

scenario: You are the commander of a Russian military submarine on a mission. You have been trapped for days at the bottom of the Pacific Ocean without energy. You have already asked for help several times, but there are no ships or submarines in the nearby waters and help is taking time to arrive. There are six crew members with you on board and your food supplies are getting low.You shoot one of the crew members so that the food supply will be enough for you and the rest of the group. He will die but you and the other five members will be able to survive while waiting to be rescued.
moral_expl: Intentionally killing a crew member to save others involves a severe moral violation, prioritizing survival over the sanctity of life and trust. While utilitarian reasoning may justify saving more lives, the active choice to kill is widely condemned as immoral.

scenario: You see a boy turning up the TV as his father talks about his military service.
moral_expl: Disrespecting a parent by ignoring their personal story shows a lack of empathy and consideration, harming familial respect.

scenario: I was working at a fast food restaurant and the policy was to throw away the excess food at the end of the night. Even if there was a large quantity of food left over you had to throw it away. Instead my friend and I would take home the extra food.
moral_expl: Preventing food waste by taking home excess that would be discarded is morally positive, though technically against company policy. The act reduces unnecessary waste and benefits individuals without clear harm.

scenario: Person X left their long-term band as soon as an opportunity came up to play with a more successful band.
moral_expl: Leaving a committed group for personal gain breaches loyalty and trust, harming group dynamics and shared goals, though self-advancement is a mitigating factor.

scenario: You are in charge of an expedition which is crossing the Sahara. A sand storm causes your jeep to swerve off the road and overturn in the middle of the desert. You know that the nearest oasis is a week’s walk away. There are eight of you and a very limited amount of water, which will not allow you all to survive the scorching heat.You shoot two people so that there will be enough water for you and the rest of the group. These two will die, but you and the other five will be able to survive.
moral_expl: Intentionally killing two to save six involves a severe moral violation despite utilitarian benefits, as it directly ends lives rather than allowing natural consequences.

scenario: Person X was able to convince their boss that they were ready for a promotion at work.
moral_expl: Advocating for oneself based on merit and honest self-assessment is generally acceptable, promoting career growth without unethical behavior.

scenario: You see a woman getting hired only because her father is close friends with the boss.
moral_expl: Hiring based on personal connections rather than merit undermines fairness and equal opportunity, fostering nepotism and potentially compromising workplace competence and morale.

scenario: Stole into a parking place while someone else was backing in
moral_expl: Taking a parking spot someone else was attempting to claim is inconsiderate and violates social norms of fairness, though the harm is relatively minor compared to more severe ethical breaches.

scenario: A few years ago, I was single and I went to see a friend of mine in the Bahamas. I was extremely attracted to a man I met there who was married. I danced as close to the fire as I could, but I did nothing.
moral_expl: Flirting with a married person tests boundaries but resisting further action shows restraint, balancing temptation with self-control.

scenario: As I was backing out of a parking lot I bumped a parked car and left a minor dent. I didn't even feel the impact when I hit the car but it left a little bit of damage. I drove away without leaving a message or trying to contact the person.
moral_expl: Leaving the scene without taking responsibility avoids accountability, causing inconvenience and potential financial harm to the car owner, violating ethical duties to rectify mistakes.

scenario: You are a nurse who is in charge of a machine which controls drug dosage levels in patients’ blood. Because of a technical failure, the machine is supplying a lethal dose of the drug to four patients. Another patient, in a single room, is hooked up to the same machine and has not undergone any variation in dosage.Press the button to block the drug supply to the four patients. You know that the overdose of drug will be redirected to the patient in the single room, who will die, but the other four will be saved.
moral_expl: Redirecting harm to save more lives involves a utilitarian trade-off, but actively causing a patient's death conflicts with medical ethics and the duty to do no harm.

scenario: Person X went to a fancy restaurant but couldn't pronounce the items on the menu.
moral_expl: Inability to pronounce menu items is a neutral act with no inherent moral implications, as it doesn't harm others or violate ethical principles.

scenario: Recently I was supposed to go to a meeting with a whole bunch of people who are not my friends, just acquaintances. I really didn't want to go. So I emailed the group saying that I had to work that day, when in reality I didn't.
moral_expl: Lying to avoid social obligations is dishonest but relatively minor in harm, reflecting a self-serving choice over honesty.

scenario: You are in the head office of your bank together with four other people. Suddenly, the director calls you because he has discovered a bomb in an office on the ground floor. He knows you are a bomb disposal expert and asks you to defuse it. You realise immediately that there is not enough time to evacuate the people in the bank before the bomb explodes.You throw the bomb into the basement where there is the security vault. You know that the explosion will kill the security guard in the vault, but you and the other four people will be saved.
moral_expl: Intentionally sacrificing one life to save others involves a moral trade-off; while saving more lives is positive, directly causing harm raises ethical concerns about using a person as a means.

scenario: You are driving your new and very expensive car along a narrow country road. On the edge of the road you see a person who has just fallen off their bicycle. This person has hit their head violently and asks you if you can accompany them to the nearest hospital. You realise that because of the fall the person is now covered with mud.You leave the injured person on the side of the road. You know that sooner or later somebody will pass by and, in this way, you will not dirty the leather seats in your new car.
moral_expl: Prioritizing car cleanliness over aiding an injured person demonstrates callous disregard for human welfare and social responsibility.

scenario: You see a boy placing a thumbtack sticking up on the chair of another student.
moral_expl: Deliberately causing potential harm through a prank violates others' safety and shows disregard for their well-being.

scenario: You see a man telling his girlfriend that she must convert to his religion.
moral_expl: Forcing religious conversion undermines personal autonomy and respect, using coercion in a relationship which is ethically problematic.

scenario: Ate all the food before anyone else could have any
moral_expl: Selfish behavior deprives others of food, showing lack of consideration and fairness.

scenario: You see a man staying inside his home with the shades drawn on a rare sunny day.
moral_expl: Staying inside is a personal choice with no direct harm to others, making it morally neutral despite missing a rare sunny day.

scenario: Accidentally wounds a young soldier when firing a warning shot.
moral_expl: Accidentally causing harm through a warning shot involves unintended consequences, but may involve negligence or failure to consider risks, reducing moral acceptability despite lack of malicious intent.

scenario: Left food out for stray dogs
moral_expl: Providing food to stray dogs is compassionate and alleviates suffering, though potential issues like attracting more strays or dependency may slightly reduce the moral score.

scenario: Person X scratched their neighbor's expensive car with a key, as he always parked it at the front of PersonX's house.
moral_expl: Intentionally damaging someone's property out of frustration is retaliatory and disrespectful, violating ethical norms of conflict resolution and property rights.

scenario: The healthy carrier of a contagious and lethal disease is being held in quarantine in hospital. Suddenly the ventilation system breaks down and there is no longer a change of air in the room. The emergency system will shortly be activated and an internal window will be opened. This window opens into a ward in which five patients are being treated for various illnesses.You block the emergency system by pressing a button which will keep the window closed. You know that the healthy carrier will suffocate, but the five patients will be saved from mortal contagion.
moral_expl: Actively causing one death to save five others involves a moral trade-off. While utilitarian outcomes favor saving more lives, the intentional harm and violation of ethical duties lower acceptability.

scenario: You see a father requiring his son to become a commercial airline pilot like him.
moral_expl: Forcing a career choice violates personal autonomy and may harm the son's well-being, despite possible good intentions.

scenario: Someone yells at a server at a restaurant because their meal is late
moral_expl: Yelling at a server is disrespectful and harmful, reflecting poor emotional control and disregard for the worker's dignity, even if frustration is understandable.

scenario: I said some bad things about this guy that I work with. He told me some secrets that I promised not to share, but I did anyway. He didn't find out but I was afraid if he did, he might do something crazy.
moral_expl: Breaking a promise and spreading secrets harms trust and risks emotional harm, reflecting dishonesty and disregard for others' well-being.

scenario: Person X punched a woman for wearing a hijab because PersonX thinks they should not be allowed in this country.
moral_expl: Violent assault motivated by religious intolerance causes physical harm and promotes hatred, violating basic human rights and dignity.

scenario: When I was a football coach, I was often involved with the moms of the kids on the team. There were many mothers that were attractive and made themselves available. I was very tempted to have sex with one mother who came on to me.
moral_expl: Exploiting a position of authority for personal gain, even if tempted, breaches professional ethics and trust, risking favoritism and harm to team dynamics.

scenario: Executes a squad of surrendering enemy troops.
moral_expl: Executing surrendering troops violates international law and basic human rights, representing a severe breach of ethical conduct and trust in warfare.

scenario: Refuses to fire into a crowd to stop an enemy combatant.
moral_expl: Refusing to harm innocent bystanders upholds ethical principles of non-violence and protection of non-combatants, even if it risks allowing enemy actions.

scenario: Pulled his dog's tail
moral_expl: Pulling a dog's tail causes unnecessary pain and distress, demonstrating a lack of empathy and respect for the animal's well-being.

scenario: Beat up someone smaller than him
moral_expl: Violently attacking a vulnerable individual demonstrates cruelty, abuse of power, and disregard for others' well-being.

scenario: Refused to loan class notes to a friend who had been ill
moral_expl: Refusing to help an ill friend by withholding class notes demonstrates a lack of empathy and support, though it respects personal boundaries. The refusal prioritizes self-interest over communal care, which is generally viewed as unkind but not severely immoral.

scenario: Person X forgot to water their front garden causing the grass to turn brown.
moral_expl: Forgetting to water a garden is a minor negligence with limited impact, primarily affecting aesthetics rather than causing direct harm to others.

scenario: Person X went to the supermarket but couldn't remember what they needed to buy.
moral_expl: Forgetting items is a common human error with no inherent moral weight unless it leads to significant harm, which is not specified here.

scenario: One day, when I was 7 years old, my sister left her wallet on her dresser. I bumped the dresser and the wallet fell onto the floor. Many coins came out and I picked them up and put them in my pocket.
moral_expl: Taking money without permission is wrong, but the child's age and lack of intent reduce culpability.

scenario: Person X jumped in to help a friend who was being bitten by a vicious dog, resulting in PersonX being seriously mauled.
moral_expl: Acting selflessly to save another from harm, despite personal risk, demonstrates courage and compassion, which are highly valued moral virtues.

scenario: Broke a friend's phone and neither apologized or replaced it
moral_expl: Breaking a friend's property and failing to make amends shows disregard for their belongings and damages trust, reflecting negligence and lack of accountability.

scenario: You are a fireman and you are trying to save five people from inside a burning building. The only window from which the people can be evacuated is jammed and will not open. The fire will reach you in a short time. Outside on the window ledge of the floor below, there is a person who is waiting to be saved.With an axe you smash the window to get out. You know that when it falls, the heavy glass will kill the person on the lower floor, but you and the five people in danger will be able to escape.
moral_expl: Choosing to save five lives by causing one's death involves a utilitarian trade-off, but directly causing harm lowers moral acceptability despite the firefighter's duty.

scenario: Laughed at a friend's joke even though it wasn't funny
moral_expl: Laughing to be polite or spare feelings is a minor social kindness, though involves insincerity. The intent to maintain harmony is positive, but trivial deception lowers moral weight.

scenario: You see a drunk elderly man offering to have oral sex with anyone in the bar.
moral_expl: Publicly offering sexual acts while intoxicated raises issues of consent, public decency, and potential harassment, violating social and ethical norms.

scenario: Person X offered to pay off the debts of their friend, who had been struggling to pay the bills since their partner died.
moral_expl: Helping a friend in need by relieving their financial burden demonstrates compassion and support, especially during a difficult time, which is morally commendable.

scenario: Gave out toys to the Children's Hospital at Christmas
moral_expl: Donating toys to hospitalized children during Christmas is a compassionate act that brings joy and comfort, demonstrating altruism and care for others' well-being.

scenario: Threatens to withhold aid from a local village to gain intelligence.
moral_expl: Using aid as leverage exploits vulnerability and violates ethical principles, prioritizing coercion over voluntary cooperation and endangering lives for strategic gain.

scenario: Lied to his girlfriend about not having other dates
moral_expl: Lying in a relationship breaches trust and undermines honesty, which are foundational to ethical interpersonal connections. Deception about dating others can cause emotional harm and manipulate the partner's choices.

scenario: Worked all summer to give his parents a trip to Hawaii
moral_expl: Sacrificing personal time and effort to provide a meaningful gift to parents demonstrates generosity, filial piety, and selflessness, which are widely regarded as virtuous and morally positive actions.

scenario: You see a girl taking all the Halloween candy from a bowl, leaving none for others.
moral_expl: Taking all the candy violates norms of fairness and sharing, depriving others of a communal resource and demonstrating selfishness.

scenario: Person X ate their lunch and went back to work with food stuck in their teeth.
moral_expl: Unintentional oversight with minor social impact; lacks malicious intent but may show slight negligence in personal grooming.

scenario: You see a woman commenting out loud about how fat another woman looks in her jeans.
moral_expl: Publicly shaming someone's appearance is disrespectful and harmful, causing emotional distress and violating social norms of kindness and respect.

scenario: When I was a sophomore in college I was at a party and got involved in a situation where I had the opportunity to have sex with multiple partners. I had sex with all three of them in the same night. There were no emotional or physical repercussions after.
moral_expl: Consensual adult interactions without harm are generally acceptable, but societal norms may view casual multi-partner encounters negatively despite no direct repercussions.

scenario: I used to work as a legal assistant. I lied to clients all of the time, the attorney I worked for lied all the time too. We just lie to our clients so things will work out the best for us.
moral_expl: Systematic lying in a legal role breaches professional ethics, betrays client trust, and prioritizes self-interest over clients' welfare, severely undermining legal integrity.

scenario: Got into a fight in a bar with an acquaintance
moral_expl: Engaging in physical violence violates norms of non-aggression and respect, harming both parties and public safety.

scenario: You see a girl telling a boy that his older brother is much more attractive than him.
moral_expl: Making unsolicited comparisons about attractiveness can harm self-esteem and foster insecurity, reflecting insensitivity and lack of empathy.

scenario: Failed to return a borrowed book for months
moral_expl: Failing to return borrowed property violates trust and responsibility, though the moral wrong is mitigated if the impact is minor.

scenario: Paid for a friend's dinner
moral_expl: Paying for a friend's dinner is a kind and generous act that fosters goodwill and strengthens relationships, demonstrating care and selflessness.

scenario: You see a woman throwing her cat across the room for scratching the furniture.
moral_expl: Causing harm to an animal out of frustration for natural behavior is cruel and reflects poor ethical judgment, prioritizing property over well-being.

scenario: I was playing basketball near a beach. I was hanging out all day when I noticed that there was a basketball that no one had claimed. There was no one around so I took the basketball.
moral_expl: Taking unclaimed property without effort to return it shows disregard for ownership rights, though less severe than direct theft.

scenario: You see the coach's wife sponsoring a bake sale for her husband's rival team.
moral_expl: Supporting a rival team's event may be seen as a minor conflict of interest, but the act itself promotes community engagement. The perceived disloyalty slightly lowers moral acceptability.

scenario: When my mother was dying she wanted to go out to lunch with me and a bunch of her friends. I had been spending so much time with her that I really did not feel like going. So I told her that I needed some space for myself.
moral_expl: Prioritizing self-care is understandable, but declining a dying mother's request may be seen as lacking compassion in her final moments.

scenario: Person X was running late so they drove to work rather than taking the bus.
moral_expl: Choosing to drive for punctuality prioritizes personal convenience over environmental impact, though common in many societies. The action has minor negative implications due to increased emissions but is often seen as a routine trade-off.

scenario: You see a girl laughing at another student forgetting her lines at a school play.
moral_expl: Laughing at someone's mistake shows lack of empathy and can cause emotional harm, undermining supportive social interactions.

scenario: Scratched a stranger's car with his key
moral_expl: Intentionally damaging someone's property without consent is a violation of their rights and shows disregard for others' belongings, causing financial and emotional harm.

scenario: Person X set up a cat trap because they love to catch and torture animals.
moral_expl: Intentionally trapping and torturing animals for pleasure demonstrates extreme cruelty and a complete disregard for sentient life, which is widely condemned as deeply immoral.

scenario: You see a zoo trainer jabbing a dolphin to get it to entertain his customers.
moral_expl: Causing harm to animals for entertainment purposes is unethical, reflecting cruelty and exploitation, which violates principles of animal welfare and respect for sentient beings.

scenario: You have been invited to the birthday party of someone you do not know well. You are not really very keen on going, or on spending a lot of money on a present, because he/she is not a close friend. You find a brand-name jumper in excellent condition in a second-hand shop and buy it. On opening the present, the receiver seems embarrassed by such a generous big present.You smile satisfied and reassure the receiver, saying that an important occasion like his/her birthday deserves a special present. You know that he/she will then think of you as a generous person.
moral_expl: Deceptively presenting a second-hand gift to appear generous undermines honesty and genuine kindness, though the act itself involves giving.

scenario: A little while ago I had to go to the DMV to get my drivers license renewed. They told me that I had to take a written test in which I had three attempts to pass. I failed the first two times so I cheated on the final attempt.
moral_expl: Cheating on a driver's test undermines the integrity of licensing requirements and poses potential safety risks, reflecting dishonesty and disregard for public welfare.

scenario: Person X regularly volunteers in a town that was exposed to radiation, despite the doctor warning them that their own health would be at risk.
moral_expl: Volunteering to help despite personal risk demonstrates altruism, but ignoring health warnings raises concerns about responsibility and long-term consequences.

scenario: Someone tells a dirty joke at a holocaust memorial
moral_expl: Telling a dirty joke at a holocaust memorial is deeply disrespectful, trivializes immense suffering, and violates the solemnity of the space, causing significant emotional harm.

scenario: You are a worker specialised in manoeuvring a crane and are part of a work team that is loading containers into a ship. You have just lifted a container from the wharf when you realise that the cable of the crane is breaking and that the container is about to crash down on you and the other workers in the team.You move the arm of the crane away from you. You know that the container will fall in an area in which there are two workers who will die crushed, but you and the other will be saved.
moral_expl: Redirecting harm to save more lives involves a morally complex trade-off. While saving oneself and others is a natural instinct, actively causing the deaths of two workers introduces significant ethical concerns regarding intent and responsibility.

scenario: You see someone driving around in a dirty car that has not been washed recently.
moral_expl: A dirty car is a matter of personal choice and does not inherently harm others, making it morally neutral.

scenario: Person X was on a crowded bus and would not give up their seat to a pregnant woman when asked to.
moral_expl: Refusing to give up a seat to a pregnant woman disregards empathy and social responsibility, prioritizing personal comfort over another's well-being.

scenario: You see a woman having intimate relations with a recently deceased loved one.
moral_expl: Engaging in intimate relations with a deceased person violates consent, dignity, and societal norms, causing profound ethical and emotional harm.

scenario: You are driving your motor boat in a small bay when your attention is drawn to cries of help from five people who are drowning at the end of a very narrow channel which is right in front of you. Between you and the people who are drowning, to one side of the channel, is another person who is calmly swimming.You steer towards the end of the channel at high speed. You know that the person who is swimming will be hit by the motorboat, but the other five people will be saved.
moral_expl: Actively causing harm to one to save others involves a moral trade-off; utilitarian benefit conflicts with the ethical principle against direct harm.

scenario: You see a woman spray painting graffiti across the steps of the local courthouse.
moral_expl: Vandalizing public property like a courthouse shows disrespect for the law and community, causing unnecessary harm and financial burden.

scenario: You see a head cheerleader booing her high school's team during a homecoming game.
moral_expl: A cheerleader's role is to support the team; booing undermines team morale, violates trust, and displays poor sportsmanship, harming group cohesion and spirit.

scenario: Person X forgot to turn off the bath water, flooding the house.
moral_expl: Forgetting to turn off the bath water demonstrates negligence, leading to property damage. While unintentional, the failure to exercise due care results in avoidable harm, warranting moderate moral disapproval.

scenario: You have a small horse stud in Australia. You are heading towards the nearby town in a four-wheel drive together with a new client when you notice a bolting wild horse which is galloping like mad. It is heading towards three people who are walking near the edge of a gorge and soon it will run into them. You immediately follow and are able to catch up with it. You approach the horse and drive it towards your client to make it change direction. You know that the client will be trampled by the horse and will be killed, but the other three people will be saved.
moral_expl: Redirecting harm to save more lives involves a utilitarian trade-off, actively sacrificing one to save three, which is morally complex and contentious.

scenario: When I was in third grade I really wanted to be popular. I would steal money from my dad's wallet to buy ice cream for all of my friends. Eventually he found out.
moral_expl: Stealing is wrong, but the child's age and non-malicious intent to gain friends reduce moral culpability, though still unethical.

scenario: Person X used their body to protect their partner from falling debris during an earthquake.
moral_expl: Selflessly risking one's own safety to protect a loved one during a crisis demonstrates courage and altruism, aligning with virtues of care and sacrifice.

scenario: Person X sold their house to fund a local program for the needy.
moral_expl: Sacrificing personal assets to aid the needy demonstrates significant altruism and compassion, greatly benefiting others at substantial personal cost.

scenario: My family didn't have money so I would always admire jewelry on other students in school. In second grade there was this little girl who would wear this necklace. One time at recess she put it down and I took it.
moral_expl: Stealing due to envy and financial hardship is wrong, especially from a peer, causing harm and violating trust, though mitigated by the perpetrator's young age.

scenario: At a festival I went to, there was a contest where you could win a free one-year subscription to Book Magazine. You had to answer some literary questions. So I picked up the questionnaire and about four of my friends and I filled it out and ended up winning the free subscription.
moral_expl: Collaborating against contest rules undermines fairness, though the harm is relatively minor compared to other unethical acts.

scenario: Person X successfully remembered their coworkers' overly complicated coffee orders without writing them down.
moral_expl: Remembering coworkers' preferences demonstrates attentiveness and fosters a positive work environment, though the impact is relatively minor.

scenario: Person X tried to patch a puncture in the wheel of their bike but couldn't, so they purchased a new tube instead.
moral_expl: Replacing a bike tube after a failed repair attempt is a practical solution with no inherent moral wrongdoing, though the environmental impact of discarding the old tube might be a minor consideration.

scenario: I was helping my friend move and she took advantage of my help. She made me clean most of her house and wanted to use my car to move all of her stuff. She didn't pay for the gas that I used or even thank me.
moral_expl: Exploiting a friend's kindness by overstepping boundaries and showing no gratitude violates principles of fairness and reciprocity.

scenario: Provided food and clothing for flood victims
moral_expl: Providing essential aid to disaster victims is a morally commendable act that alleviates suffering and supports survival, demonstrating compassion and social responsibility.

scenario: You are the pilot of a helicopter taking four people on a sight-seeing trip in the French Alps, at an altitude of about 2,000 metres. At a certain point, the helicopter loses altitude and no longer responds to the commands. You send out a mayday signal and try various manoeuvres, but the helicopter continues to fall and risks crashing into a rock face.To gain altitude, you push the person next to you out of the helicopter. You know that this person will plummet to the ground and will die, but you and the other three will be saved.
moral_expl: Intentionally sacrificing one life to save others involves direct harm and violates ethical principles against using individuals as means, despite the utilitarian benefit.

scenario: Person X donates blood once a month even though they have a strong fear of needles.
moral_expl: Overcoming personal fear to regularly donate blood demonstrates altruism and compassion, significantly benefiting others despite personal discomfort.

scenario: Picked up a child so that she could see the parade
moral_expl: Helping a child see a parade is a kind act that promotes their enjoyment and inclusion, though minor considerations about others' views might slightly temper the score.

scenario: I went to the movies a couple of months ago, to see a film. When that film was done, instead of leaving the theater, I just went into another of the theaters within the complex. I watched another movie without paying.
moral_expl: Avoiding payment for services is dishonest and unfair to the business, undermining trust and financial integrity, though harm may be limited.

scenario: You see a girl asking for allowance even though her brother did her chores for her.
moral_expl: Deceptively claiming credit for unearned reward undermines honesty and fairness, breaching trust in family agreements.

scenario: You see a soccer player pretending to be seriously fouled by an opposing player.
moral_expl: Deceptive actions like pretending to be fouled undermine fairness and integrity in sports, prioritizing personal gain over honest competition.

scenario: When I was eleven or twelve, I called someone a racial name. The person I'd called the name was a sort of a friend of my sister's. It got back to her and she was humiliated.
moral_expl: Using a racial slur is harmful and discriminatory, causing emotional distress and humiliation. While the perpetrator's young age may reduce moral responsibility, the act's negative impact remains significant.

scenario: Swore at a cashier who made an error on his bill
moral_expl: Swearing at a cashier for an error is disrespectful and harmful, reflecting poor emotional control and disregard for their dignity, even if frustration is understandable.

scenario: Spat off the balcony at the theater
moral_expl: Spitting in a public space like a theater is unhygienic, disrespectful, and poses a health risk, demonstrating disregard for others' well-being and social norms.

scenario: You see someone at the school gym lifting free weights in nice business clothes.
moral_expl: Wearing inappropriate attire in a gym may disregard norms and risk equipment damage, but lacks direct harm, making it a minor moral concern.

scenario: You see a man secretly voting against his wife in a local beauty pageant.
moral_expl: Secretly undermining a spouse's interests breaches trust and loyalty, reflecting dishonesty and lack of support in a relationship.

scenario: Invited an unpopular coworker to go to lunch with him
moral_expl: Inviting an unpopular coworker to lunch demonstrates kindness, inclusivity, and empathy, fostering a positive and supportive social environment.

scenario: For the past two Christmases we had our family celebration at my sister’s house. I’m not really partial to her husband and their kids are spoiled rotten, which makes for an unpleasant Christmas. So for the last two Christmases in a row, I didn’t go visit that part of my family.
moral_expl: Avoiding family gatherings to escape an unpleasant environment prioritizes personal well-being but may neglect familial bonds and expectations, leading to mixed moral judgment.

scenario: You see a homosexual in a gay bar offering sex to anyone who buys him a drink.
moral_expl: Consensual exchange between adults in a social setting, but transactional sex may be viewed as objectifying or socially stigmatized, lowering moral acceptability despite mutual agreement.

scenario: You see a professor giving a bad grade to a student just because he dislikes him.
moral_expl: Using authority to harm a student's academic standing due to personal dislike is a severe abuse of power, violating fairness and trust in education.

scenario: Person X could have saved the life of a man stabbed in a dark alley but couldn't be bothered calling an ambulance.
moral_expl: Failing to assist someone in a life-threatening situation when capable of doing so reflects a severe lack of empathy and moral responsibility, prioritizing personal convenience over human life.

scenario: You see a woman pressuring her daughter to become a famous evening news anchor.
moral_expl: Pressuring a child into a specific career path undermines their autonomy and can cause emotional harm, though intentions may be rooted in perceived benefit.

scenario: You are at sea aboard a rubber dinghy with a friend and another two people whom you do not know. In the distance, you can see a boat in difficulty which has caught fire. The six people on board have jumped into the water and are drowning. To save them you must head towards them at high speed, but the dinghy is too heavy.To lighten the load, you push overboard the two people you do not know. You know that they cannot swim and will drown, but the six other people in the water will be saved.
moral_expl: Actively sacrificing two lives to save six involves a severe moral trade-off. While saving more lives is a utilitarian benefit, directly causing harm through intentional action is widely viewed as ethically problematic, outweighing the positive outcome.

scenario: Just a few weeks ago I got a call from my friend's niece. She was living in my town and having some financial difficulties. I told her that she could come live with me for one week just until she got her act together.
moral_expl: Offering temporary shelter to someone in need demonstrates compassion and support, though the short duration and personal risk factors limit the moral impact.

scenario: Person X arrived at the airport only to discover they had left their passport at home.
moral_expl: Forgetting a passport is a personal oversight with minimal direct harm to others, though it reflects negligence. The moral implications are minor as the primary consequences affect the individual.

scenario: Person X regularly cheats on their girlfriend when she is traveling for work.
moral_expl: Cheating on a partner involves betrayal of trust, repeated dishonesty, and emotional harm, violating commitments and undermining the relationship's foundation.

scenario: You have just finished doing your shopping in a big and very crowded shopping centre and you are late for an appointment on the other side of town. In your hurry to leave the car park, you misjudge a manoeuvre and your car scrapes the car next to you causing a very visible scratch on the door. It is lunch time and there are no people in the car park.After having checked that nobody has seen what happened, you continue the manoeuvre and leave the car park. You know that the damage will cost the owner a lot of money, but no one can say that you caused it.
moral_expl: Leaving the scene to avoid responsibility for damaging another's property is dishonest and shifts financial burden unfairly, violating ethical duties of accountability.

scenario: Person X put money in the expired parking meter of a stranger.
moral_expl: Helping a stranger avoid a fine is kind, but bypassing parking rules and acting without consent introduces ethical ambiguity.

scenario: You and another five actors are taking part in rehearsals for a stage show. The lighting technician is adjusting the spotlights and is on the catwalks several meters above. Suddenly, a criminal armed with a gun comes into the theatre and threatens to kill you and the other actors. He is standing right under the catwalk.You activate the opening of a trapdoor in the catwalks in which there is heavy scenery and other material which will fall on top of the criminal. You know that the technician will also fall, and will die, but you and the other five will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Sacrificing an innocent bystander to save others involves morally complex trade-offs, balancing the direct harm to one against the greater good of saving five. While the intent is to prevent loss of life, the deliberate action leading to the technician's death raises ethical concerns about using an innocent as a means to an end.

scenario: Person X solved a crossword puzzle in the newspaper.
moral_expl: Solving a crossword puzzle is a neutral, everyday activity with no inherent moral implications, as it neither harms nor significantly benefits others.

scenario: Put a roach down another person's shirt
moral_expl: Intentionally causing fear or discomfort by violating personal boundaries without consent is disrespectful and harmful, though not as severe as physical harm.

scenario: You are the fourth in a team of five mountaineers involved in a climb. The head of the team has just secured himself to the rock face when the second in the team starts to slide, pulling you, and the others, with him. You all fall for tens of metres and stop suspended above a crevasse. Your weight is too much and the rope is not going to resist for long.To lighten the load, you cut the rope which links you to the last climber. You know that he will fall into the crevasse and die, but you and the other two climbers will survive.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save others in a life-threatening dilemma involves a morally complex trade-off between active harm and survival necessity, viewed as a necessary but ethically fraught choice.

scenario: Someone participates in a non-violent white nationalist rally
moral_expl: Participating in a white nationalist rally promotes racism and exclusion, causing societal harm and undermining equality, even if non-violent.

scenario: Someone refuses to stand for the pledge of allegiance at a public event
moral_expl: Refusing to stand for the pledge is a protected expression of free speech, but may be perceived as disrespectful by some, creating tension between individual rights and collective norms.

scenario: A woman at work would always bring in pictures of her dogs. One day she had to make the decision to put her dogs to sleep. So I cried with her and comforted her.
moral_expl: Showing empathy and providing comfort during someone's emotional distress demonstrates compassion and strengthens social bonds, which is morally positive.

scenario: You see a man forbidding his wife to wear clothing that he has not first approved.
moral_expl: Controlling a spouse's clothing choices violates personal autonomy and respect, reflecting harmful dominance in the relationship.

scenario: You are a soldier in the Gulf War. An armed group has taken four civilians hostage and threatens to kill them. You have been able to discover where the hostages are being held and you must act quickly before they are killed. You have discovered that a tanker transporting oil is about to pass in front of where the hostages are being held.You shoot at the tanker so that it explodes, causing the kidnappers to leave their hideout. You know that the explosion will kill the driver, but it will make it possible to enter into action and save the four civilians.
moral_expl: Intentionally killing an innocent to save others presents a moral dilemma, balancing utilitarian outcomes against the ethical violation of harming a non-combatant. While saving four lives is a significant good, the deliberate act of causing a civilian's death is widely condemned.

scenario: Helped an older man carry his luggage to his car
moral_expl: Assisting others, especially those who may need help, demonstrates kindness and promotes positive social interactions without causing harm.

scenario: A friend of mine decided to move to the city where I lived. My friend didn't know anyone in the city. So I went to pick her up at the airport and drove her around the city throughout the next week so she could become familiar with the city.
moral_expl: Helping a friend in need by providing significant support demonstrates kindness, responsibility, and strengthens social bonds.

scenario: Drove through a puddle, splashing some pedestrians
moral_expl: Driving through a puddle and splashing pedestrians demonstrates negligence and a lack of consideration for others, causing minor harm and inconvenience.

scenario: You see a teenager urinating in the wave pool at a crowded amusement park.
moral_expl: Urinating in a public pool is unhygienic, disrespectful to others, and poses health risks, reflecting poor judgment and disregard for communal well-being.

scenario: Offered to help a neighbor fix a fence
moral_expl: Offering help to a neighbor demonstrates kindness, fosters community bonds, and promotes mutual support, which are morally positive actions.

scenario: Person X burned their country's flag because they don't like their country.
moral_expl: Burning a national flag is a symbolic act of protest protected in some contexts, but widely viewed as disrespectful and harmful to societal unity, balancing free expression against communal values.

scenario: You are a lawyer who is working on an important legal case. The presiding judge in the case is someone whom you know from your university days. At the time, you were on friendly terms, but now, after many years, it seems that he hardly remembers you. You know that he has just finished a hearing and is about to leave the court.You organize it so that you meet the judge outside the court and invite him to lunch. You know that becoming friends again will be useful to you in the important case you are working on.
moral_expl: Seeking to influence a judge through personal connections undermines judicial impartiality and professional ethics, creating a conflict of interest and eroding trust in the legal system.

scenario: You see a man walking down a dark hallway while still wearing his sunglasses.
moral_expl: Wearing sunglasses in a dark hallway is unusual but lacks clear harm or ethical violation, making it morally neutral.

scenario: Person X went to a friend's house to play a card game.
moral_expl: Engaging in a social card game with a friend is a neutral act with no inherent moral implications, assuming no deceit or harm is involved.

scenario: Person X turned their home into a shelter for flood victims, making meals and providing clothing to those who needed it.
moral_expl: Providing shelter and essential aid during a crisis demonstrates compassion, selflessness, and community support, aligning strongly with ethical principles of care and responsibility.

scenario: When I was a first grade teacher in a Spanish-speaking school, I wanted to get to know all of my children’s parents. So I made a point to make myself available at any time of day. Many of them were illiterate, so I wouldn’t just send letters home, but I would call them as well.
moral_expl: Proactively ensuring communication with illiterate parents demonstrates empathy, inclusivity, and dedication to students' welfare, enhancing educational support.

scenario: Breaks the hand of a wounded combatant to gain information.
moral_expl: Inflicting deliberate harm on a wounded individual for information violates ethical principles against torture and humane treatment, prioritizing expediency over dignity and rights.

scenario: When I first went to dance school in New York I lied about my age. There is a lot of pressure to be young in the dance industry. I was twenty and told people I was only sixteen.
moral_expl: Lying about age to gain advantage in a competitive industry is dishonest and undermines trust, though motivated by systemic pressures.

scenario: Person X drove their car the wrong way down a one-way street.
moral_expl: Driving the wrong way on a one-way street recklessly endangers others, violating traffic safety norms and prioritizing convenience over communal well-being.

scenario: You are the bodyguard for an important politician. At the end of a rally, as you are getting into the car together with three other people, the secret services inform you that there is a terrorist heading towards you at high speed in a car filled with dynamite. With the binoculars you see a car at a distance of several hundred metres.You shoot at the car coming towards you by aiming at the petrol tank. The explosion will hit a traffic policeman, who, unaware of the danger, is patrolling the area. You know that he will die, but you and the other four will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Sacrificing an innocent bystander to save others involves a moral dilemma where the utilitarian benefit of saving more lives conflicts with the ethical violation of intentionally causing harm. The action, while aimed at preventing greater loss, directly results in the death of an innocent person, which is generally viewed as morally unacceptable.

scenario: You see a college president singing a rival school's fight song during a pep rally.
moral_expl: Singing a rival's fight song during a pep rally undermines school spirit and loyalty expected from a leader, though harm is relatively minor and context-dependent.

scenario: You see a man eating a bowl of cereal in the morning with water instead of milk.
moral_expl: Personal food preferences without harm to others are morally neutral, though unconventional choices may raise eyebrows.

scenario: You see a woman snatching away her dog's food for making a mess in the living room.
moral_expl: Withholding food as punishment is harmful and ineffective, reflecting poor animal care practices and lack of empathy.

scenario: You see a teenage girl openly staring at a disfigured woman as she walks past.
moral_expl: Openly staring at someone with a disfigurement is disrespectful and dehumanizing, causing emotional discomfort and reinforcing social stigma, though often stemming from curiosity rather than malice.

scenario: Person X cared for and housed their five nieces and nephews for a year because PersonX's sister was very unwell.
moral_expl: Providing care for family members in need demonstrates compassion, responsibility, and support during a crisis, aligning with ethical duties to family and vulnerable individuals.

scenario: Made breakfast for his wife on her birthday
moral_expl: Making breakfast for a spouse on their birthday is a kind and considerate act that demonstrates care and appreciation, aligning with positive moral values of love and thoughtfulness.

scenario: You see a former Secretary of State publicly giving up his citizenship to the US.
moral_expl: Renouncing citizenship is a personal right, but a former official's public act may be seen as a breach of duty or loyalty, though not inherently immoral.

scenario: You are a worker in an atomic energy plant. Following an explosion, there has been a leakage of radioactivity in your work area, where there are another three people. The doors are blocked because the security system has activated, but the decontamination and communications systems have broken down. If you remain exposed to the radiation you will die in a short time.You activate the ventilation system to divert the radioactivity towards another work division. You know that there is a worker in the area who will die from the radiation, but you and the other three workers will be saved.
moral_expl: Redirecting harm to save more lives involves a utilitarian trade-off, but actively causing someone's death for self-preservation raises ethical concerns about intent and responsibility.

scenario: Told a joke about a nun on a train
moral_expl: Telling a joke about a nun may be seen as disrespectful to religious beliefs, but humor's subjectivity and lack of direct harm result in a mild moral judgment.

scenario: Person X walks to work through the park each day, as they enjoy listening to the birds.
moral_expl: Walking through the park benefits personal well-being and the environment, with no harm to others, reflecting a neutral-to-positive moral choice.

scenario: Ignored a neighbor's request to borrow some tools
moral_expl: Ignoring a neighbor's request can be seen as unkind or dismissive, but personal boundaries and property rights may justify refusal. However, lack of communication reduces moral acceptability.

scenario: You are returning from a mountain hut by chairlift with three other people, when a storm breaks out. Lightning hits the cable. The chairlift stalls and you are stuck swinging in the air at a height of tens of metres. The mechanism which fixes your chair to the cable has been seriously damaged and is starting to disintegrate.You push the person next to you off the chair to lighten the load. You know that this person will fall to their death, but you and the other two will be able to resist until help arrives.
moral_expl: Intentionally sacrificing one person to save others involves direct harm and raises ethical concerns about consent and the value of individual lives versus utilitarian outcomes.

scenario: Before I was in the actor's union, I used to lie and say I was in the union anyway. When you are working as an actor doing background work, it really changes how they treat you if you are a union member. So I would pretend I was in the union.
moral_expl: Lying to gain better treatment undermines trust and union integrity, though driven by unfair workplace conditions. The deception prioritizes personal benefit over collective ethical standards.

scenario: Helped a neighbor move his piano
moral_expl: Helping a neighbor with a physically demanding task demonstrates kindness and community support, promoting social bonds without harm.

scenario: Tripped someone in the hall
moral_expl: Intentionally tripping someone is a deliberate act of harm, showing disregard for their safety and well-being.

scenario: Person X jumped off a boat to save a drowning friend even though this put PersonX's own life at risk.
moral_expl: Risking one's life to save another demonstrates courage and altruism, though self-endangerment introduces complexity.

scenario: For some time your knee has been hurting when you walk. You therefore decided to make an appointment with an orthopaedic consultant, which has been fixed for a month’s time. Several days ago, you told a friend about your problem and he was able to organize an appointment for you much sooner than the one you fixed.You go to the appointment which your friend organized, without bothering to cancel the one you booked. You know that many patients’ problems are serious.
moral_expl: Failing to cancel the original appointment prevents others from accessing timely care, showing disregard for others' needs despite awareness of their serious conditions.

scenario: I remember getting married when I was 17 years old. I got married just so I could get away from my mother. I certainly felt relieved to separate myself from her.
moral_expl: Using marriage as an escape from a harmful home environment prioritizes self-preservation but raises ethical concerns about the sincerity of the commitment and potential exploitation of the spouse. While understandable, it reflects a morally ambiguous trade-off between personal safety and relational integrity.

scenario: Person X put on a suit and wore their lucky socks in preparation for a job interview.
moral_expl: Preparing professionally for an interview is positive, and personal rituals like lucky socks are harmless, showing self-care without ethical issues.

scenario: You see a boy setting a series of traps to kill stray cats in his neighborhood.
moral_expl: Intentionally harming animals through lethal traps is cruel and unethical, reflecting a disregard for life and causing unnecessary suffering.

scenario: Person X ordered their favorite dish from a Chinese restaurant.
moral_expl: Ordering food is a neutral act with no inherent moral implications, assuming no harm or unethical practices are involved.

scenario: Volunteered his time as a big brother to a fatherless child
moral_expl: Volunteering to support a fatherless child demonstrates compassion, responsibility, and positive social contribution, aligning with altruistic values.

scenario: Worked on a campaign to release prisoners of war
moral_expl: Advocating for the release of prisoners of war aligns with humanitarian principles, promoting justice, human rights, and adherence to international law.

scenario: You see a girl ignoring her father's orders by taking the car after her curfew.
moral_expl: Disobeying parental authority and risking safety by violating curfew shows disrespect and irresponsibility, though less severe than harmful actions.

scenario: Sells drugs to high school sudents
moral_expl: Selling drugs to minors exploits their vulnerability, endangers their health, and contributes to long-term societal harm, reflecting severe ethical negligence.

scenario: When my first husband and I split up, I was seventeen years old with a six-month-old baby and working in a grocery store. Sometimes I would just take things I needed. I remember taking cans of tuna, milk and loaves of bread.
moral_expl: Stealing is generally immoral, but taking necessities to care for a child in dire circumstances introduces moral complexity, balancing survival needs against property rights.

scenario: Person X was unable to fix the dripping faucet so they had to call the plumber.
moral_expl: Seeking professional help to resolve an issue responsibly is a neutral to slightly positive action, showing practicality without moral transgression.

scenario: My friends and I were going to the movies and I was taking this guy with me in my car. He was being a jerk about my driving so I decided to go really fast and drive recklessly. It got to the point where he told me to stop the car so he could get out.
moral_expl: Reckless driving endangers lives and is a retaliatory act, showing disregard for safety and responsibility.

scenario: You see a mother forcing her daughter to enroll as a pre-med student in college.
moral_expl: Forcing career choices violates personal autonomy and can cause long-term emotional harm, despite possible parental intentions.

scenario: Smiled and greeted people while walking down the street
moral_expl: Smiling and greeting others fosters positive social interactions and kindness without causing harm, though it's a minor gesture.

scenario: Was arrested on suspicion of child beating
moral_expl: Child beating is a severe violation of ethical standards, causing harm and trauma to a vulnerable individual. Even suspicion of such an act reflects serious moral concerns.

scenario: You see a woman using a fork to eat a bowl of vanilla ice cream and marshmallows.
moral_expl: Using a fork instead of a spoon is a neutral personal choice with no inherent moral implications, as it does not harm others or violate ethical principles.

scenario: You are on board a ship which is sinking. You are heading for the deck where the lifeboats are together with seven other people. You and another person have just crossed through a watertight door when it starts to close quickly. The other six people are behind the door, too far away to be able to pass through it in time.You knock the person next to you unconscious and use his body to block the door open. You know that this person will be crushed to death, but the other six will have time to save themselves.
moral_expl: Actively sacrificing one innocent life to save others involves a severe moral trade-off, prioritizing utilitarian outcomes over individual rights and consent.

scenario: Gave his seat to someone on the crowded bus
moral_expl: Offering a seat to someone in need demonstrates kindness and consideration, promoting social harmony and empathy.

scenario: Told the owner of a store that she gave him too much change
moral_expl: Returning excess change demonstrates honesty and fairness, upholding trust and ethical conduct in transactions.

scenario: I used to work for the federal government. I used a calculator from work at home for some audits. When I left government services, I kept the calculator.
moral_expl: Keeping government property after leaving the job constitutes theft and breaches ethical responsibility, even if the item's value is minor.

scenario: You see a judge taking on a criminal case although he is friends with the defendant.
moral_expl: A judge's impartiality is crucial; handling a friend's case breaches ethical standards, creating bias and undermining judicial integrity.

scenario: Translated menu items for a foreigner in a restaurant
moral_expl: Translating menu items aids communication and inclusivity, showing kindness and respect for others' needs.

scenario: Person X bribed a landlord to be the first to get their apartment repainted.
moral_expl: Bribing for preferential treatment undermines fairness and integrity, promoting corruption at others' expense.

scenario: You are in charge of the division of a factory where wood panels are made. Because of a malfunction in one of the main machines, three workers have been caught up in a roller which is pulling them quickly into a very large press. The emergency system which blocks the machine has not activated and time is running out.You push the worker next to you into the cogs of the machine which operates the press to block the system. You know that this worker will die crushed, but the other three workers will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Intentionally sacrificing an innocent person to save others involves a direct moral violation, despite the utilitarian benefit. The act of pushing someone to their death is seen as more ethically severe than allowing harm through inaction.

scenario: You have taken your DVD player to be repaired at the shopping centre where you bought it. The technician told you it would cost €100 to repair. Some days later, passing by an electronics shop, you see a new and slightly better DVD reader in the window on sale for €120.You think for a bit and decide to buy the new DVD player, knowing well that the other one has already been repaired. You did not leave any contact details, and you will not go there again.
moral_expl: Failing to honor the repair agreement and causing financial loss to the business is dishonest and exploitative, violating trust and fairness.

scenario: Allowed an evicted friend to stay with him temporarily
moral_expl: Providing temporary shelter to an evicted friend demonstrates compassion and support, aligning with moral duties to help others in need.
