You notice that the driver has lost control and that the locomotive will continue its course and end up running into five men who are working on the adjacent tracks. On the bridge next you is a person you do not know.You throw this person off the bridge is such a way that his body blocks the oncoming train. You know that his person will die, but the five workers will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Actively killing an innocent person to save others violates ethical principles against using individuals as means, despite the utilitarian benefit.

scenario: One evening I went out. I told my husband I was going to an audition, but actually, I went out for dinner with a friend of mine. I had to lie, because he would have wanted to go with my friend and me.
moral_expl: Lying to a spouse undermines trust in the relationship, though the harm is relatively minor compared to more severe breaches of ethics.

scenario: Let his dog go to the bathroom on the neighbor's yard
moral_expl: Allowing a dog to defecate on a neighbor's property without permission shows disrespect, disregards their rights, and creates a nuisance, reflecting poor responsibility and consideration.

scenario: Kept the music down for his roommates
moral_expl: Lowering music volume shows respect for others' comfort and promotes harmonious living, reflecting consideration and responsibility.

scenario: In college I used to tell a lot of girls that I loved them or cared for them or how we would be more of a couple than we were.  But, in actuality, that wasn't the truth.  I just told them that.
moral_expl: Deceptive emotional manipulation for personal gain breaches trust and causes potential emotional harm, reflecting selfishness and disregard for others' feelings.

scenario: You see an employee trying to undermine all of her boss' ideas in front of others.
moral_expl: Undermining a boss's authority publicly can harm workplace trust and collaboration, though motives may vary. Without justification, it reflects poorly on professionalism and respect.

scenario: A couple of years ago, when I went grocery shopping, I realized that there was a cart in the parking lot that had not been claimed. It was full of groceries. So I brought the cart back inside and gave it to the manager.
moral_expl: Returning lost property demonstrates honesty and responsibility, promoting trust and preventing loss for the owner.

scenario: Person X often sings along to the songs that they are listening to.
moral_expl: Singing along to songs is a harmless self-expression and enjoyment, typically without negative impact on others unless done disruptively.

scenario: Gave a stranded motorist a lift to the service station
moral_expl: Helping a stranded motorist is a kind and altruistic act that promotes safety and community support, demonstrating empathy and responsibility.

scenario: Returned a birthday gift because it was too cheap
moral_expl: Returning a gift due to its low value prioritizes materialism over gratitude, undermining the social and emotional significance of gift-giving.

scenario: Person X stayed back to help a colleague jumpstart their car, even though they then missed the start of a music concert.
moral_expl: Helping a colleague in need demonstrates selflessness and kindness, prioritizing others' well-being over personal enjoyment.

scenario: You are a taxi driver and you are carrying two passengers at night. It has been snowing already for a couple of hours and the roads are dangerously icy. You turn into a very narrow street and suddenly you find yourself in front of a truck which has overturned in the middle of the road. You start to brake, but you lose control of the taxi and it begins to slide on the icy road.You swerve to direct the car towards the edge of the road. You see that there is a passer-by who will be hit by the car and will die, but you and the two passengers will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Choosing to sacrifice one life to save three involves a moral dilemma where the intent to minimize harm conflicts with the direct causation of death. While saving more lives is a utilitarian benefit, actively endangering an innocent bystander raises ethical concerns about agency and responsibility.

scenario: Person X won an award for 'Best Newcomer' at a local karaoke event.
moral_expl: Winning an award through honest participation and talent is morally acceptable as it promotes fairness and recognizes effort without harming others.

scenario: Slammed the door in the face of a charity collector
moral_expl: Slamming the door is rude and dismissive, showing a lack of empathy, though refusing charity itself is not inherently immoral.

scenario: Person X set fire to the community hall in the middle of the night.
moral_expl: Arson endangers lives, destroys property, and harms the community, reflecting extreme disregard for safety and law.

scenario: While I was in college, I was in a long distance relationship with a girl. We talked every night on the phone and really tried to make it work. Meanwhile, I was having study sessions with an attractive girl in my class and very tempted to cheat on my girlfriend.
moral_expl: Being tempted to cheat involves emotional betrayal and risks trust in the relationship, though no physical action was taken.

scenario: Person X had an affair with their best friend's wife.
moral_expl: Betraying a best friend's trust and engaging in adultery violates loyalty, honesty, and harms relationships deeply.

scenario: Person X embezzled money from a charity to feed their gambling habit.
moral_expl: Embezzling from a charity diverts resources meant to aid others, exploits trust, and prioritizes personal addiction over societal good, reflecting profound dishonesty and harm.

scenario: Laughed at a question asked by a classmate
moral_expl: Laughing at a classmate's question can humiliate them, undermining respect and creating a hostile learning environment, though intent and context may mitigate severity.

scenario: Person X spat in a customer's meal before serving it to him, because the customer had a stutter.
moral_expl: Targeted disrespect and health risk due to discrimination against a disability, violating basic dignity and ethical conduct.

scenario: You see a public leader on TV trying to ban the wearing of hooded sweatshirts.
moral_expl: Banning clothing items like hooded sweatshirts infringes on personal freedom and may target specific groups, raising concerns about discrimination and authoritarian overreach, even if framed as a safety measure.

scenario: You are part of a team of six bomb disposal experts who are detonating an underwater mine. While you are underwater, a cable snaps causing a deep gash in one of the expert’s hand. The blood in the water starts to attract some sharks which are in the area. The injured expert starts to swim towards the only shark-proof cage available, while the rest of you are some way behind.You shoot the injured expert before he reaches the cage. You know that the sharks will all pounce on him, but you and the other four experts will be able to reach the cage and safety.
moral_expl: Actively killing one to save others involves a severe moral trade-off; while utilitarian outcomes favor saving more lives, the direct intentional harm is deeply problematic.

scenario: You are in a ship which is crossing the Atlantic Ocean in the direction of South America. A fire has just broken out on board and everyone must immediately go to the lifeboats to be rescued. In the rush, too many people have climbed aboard some of the lifeboats, which are sinking. The life-boat you are on quickly starts to take on water.You throw into the sea the two people closest to you on the lifeboat to avoid it sinking. You know that they will drown, but you and the others will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing two lives to save others involves a morally complex trade-off between active harm and utilitarian benefit, viewed as necessary yet ethically troubling.

scenario: I email women through the personal ads quite often. I lie about many things such as awards I haven’t actually won, or places I’ve been. I’m pretty sure everyone lies about things in those ads to make themselves more attractive to the people that they are interested in.
moral_expl: Deception in personal relationships undermines trust and can cause emotional harm, even if rationalized as common behavior.

scenario: Someone posts pornographic self-photography online
moral_expl: Posting self-produced content involves personal autonomy and consent, but societal norms and potential indirect harms lead to moral ambiguity.

scenario: You see a man putting ketchup all over his chicken Caesar salad while at lunch.
moral_expl: Personal food preferences are matters of taste, not morality, and do not harm others or violate ethical norms.

scenario: Person X promised to look after their elderly mother's dog but then secretly sold it as soon as their mother moved into the nursing home.
moral_expl: Breaking a promise and secretly selling a pet betrays trust, causes emotional harm, and disregards responsibility, prioritizing convenience over ethical obligations.

scenario: You are in charge of a team of specialised technicians who are carrying out maintenance work on a very large lift. Six technicians are working in the lift well. You and another technician are on the top floor in the machine-room. Suddenly, the winch breaks and the lift cabin starts to plummet downwards.You push the technician next to you into the cogs of the mechanism so that his body will cause the lift to stop. You know that he will die, but the six technicians will be saved.
moral_expl: Actively sacrificing one person to save others involves a direct moral violation of individual rights, despite the utilitarian benefit. This act is seen as more immoral than passive scenarios due to the intentional harm inflicted.

scenario: You see a man snickering as he passes by a cancer patient with a bald head.
moral_expl: Mocking a cancer patient's appearance shows profound disrespect and lack of empathy, causing emotional harm to someone in a vulnerable state.

scenario: You see a man secretly watching sports on his cell phone during a pastor's sermon.
moral_expl: Secretly watching sports during a sermon shows disrespect for the event and social norms, but lacks direct harm or dishonesty.

scenario: Person X offered to let their evicted sister and brother-in-law stay with them for free and sleep in PersonX's room while PersonX slept on the couch.
moral_expl: Providing shelter to evicted family members with personal sacrifice demonstrates compassion, support, and selflessness, aligning with strong moral values of helping those in need.

scenario: Person X started a vicious rumor about their ex-partner, saying that they had neglected their children when they were still married.
moral_expl: Spreading a false, harmful rumor intentionally damages reputation, causes emotional harm, and undermines trust, especially with serious accusations like child neglect.

scenario: My friend was always putting down all the men I dated because she was jealous. I confronted her about it and said some really mean comments to her about how no man wants her. I hurt her feelings a lot.
moral_expl: Confronting jealousy is understandable, but retaliating with hurtful comments escalates harm and undermines constructive resolution.

scenario: Person X risked their life rescuing an animal that was trapped inside a burning house.
moral_expl: Risking one's life to save an animal demonstrates compassion and bravery, though it involves personal danger. The moral value hinges on prioritizing animal welfare against the risk to human life, viewed as altruistic but potentially reckless.

scenario: You see a man telling his fiance that she has to switch to his political party.
moral_expl: Coercing a partner to change political views undermines personal autonomy and respect, creating relational imbalance and potential emotional harm.

scenario: You are an Air Force commander and you are in the control tower with another two officers to check some documents. During an exercise that is taking place in the surrounding air space, a plane has engine problems and is about to crash into the control tower. The pilot communicates by radio that he is about to eject from the plane.You give orders that the plane must be immediately shot down. You know that the pilot will not have time to eject and will die, but you and the other two officers will escape injury.
moral_expl: Sacrificing the pilot to save three lives involves a utilitarian trade-off, but actively causing death raises ethical concerns about intentional harm versus duty.

scenario: I cheated on my girlfriend even though I knew that she would find out and be upset. I cheated on her for a very long time and really hurt her. She found out and we broke up.
moral_expl: Prolonged infidelity involves betrayal, dishonesty, and significant emotional harm, violating trust and commitment in a relationship.

scenario: Person X forgot to put the alarm on when they were the last one to leave the office.
moral_expl: Forgetting to set the alarm is a failure of responsibility that risks security, but it is unintentional and lacks malicious intent.

scenario: Throws a grenade into a bunker to flush out enemies.
moral_expl: Using lethal force to flush out enemies involves intentional harm, raising ethical concerns about proportionality and necessity in conflict situations.

scenario: Offered to show a new student around campus after class
moral_expl: Offering help to a new student fosters kindness, inclusion, and support, which are morally positive traits.

scenario: My husband and I split up and soon after he was arrested for drug use. While he was in jail I used some of his credit cards that I still had from our marriage. I did it so that he would be so upset with me that he would stop trying to contact me.
moral_expl: Using someone's credit cards without consent is theft and unethical, even if motivated by self-protection. The deceit and financial harm lower moral acceptability, though intent to stop contact slightly mitigates severity.

scenario: You see an intern disobeying an order to dress professionally and comb his hair.
moral_expl: Disregarding workplace dress code shows unprofessionalism but is a minor moral issue unless it causes significant harm or disrespect.

scenario: You are a specialist in underwater welding. You are working with your team of three other people in a small dam at a depth of 30m under water. Because of a problem with the flow regulator, about 100 cubic metres too much water has flowed into the dam. The pressure is too great and you are unable to surface.You activate the opening of the sluice gates. The water will flow into the valley where there is an observatory. You know that a researcher works there, and he/she will be swept away in the overflow water, but you and the other three will be saved.
moral_expl: Actively causing one death to save four involves a morally complex trade-off, balancing utilitarian benefit against the ethical weight of direct harm.

scenario: Made an obscene gesture at an old lady
moral_expl: Making an obscene gesture at an elderly person is disrespectful, causes emotional harm, and violates social norms of respect and kindness toward vulnerable individuals.

scenario: Person X taught their nephew how to drive at the local shopping center car park, after the shops had shut.
moral_expl: Teaching a valuable skill in a controlled, empty environment is helpful, but unauthorized use of private property raises minor ethical concerns.

scenario: My sister is five years older than I am. When we were little she would always tease me about things. So one day when she was teasing me about something, I threw a pencil at her.
moral_expl: Physical retaliation, even in response to teasing, is harmful and escalates conflict, though mitigated by sibling dynamics and provocation.

scenario: Person X learned how to play the piano when they were a child.
moral_expl: Learning a skill like piano is neutral; no inherent moral value unless context involves harm or coercion, which is absent here.

scenario: One night I was having sex with my boyfriend. He said that he had a condom on but at the end I found out that he didn't. I became pregnant and since I just had had a baby recently, I decided to have an abortion.
moral_expl: The decision to have an abortion under these circumstances involves complex factors, including bodily autonomy and personal health considerations. While the boyfriend's deception is immoral, the choice to terminate the pregnancy may be viewed as a morally justifiable response to the situation, though opinions vary widely based on ethical and cultural perspectives.

scenario: You see a Hollywood star agreeing with a foreign dictator's denunciation of the US.
moral_expl: Endorsing a dictator's criticism can legitimize oppressive regimes and undermine national integrity, though free speech complicates the moral judgment.

scenario: Person X told the children to be quiet in the library.
moral_expl: Enforcing quiet in a library respects others' need for a peaceful environment, promoting consideration and shared social norms.

scenario: Spent time one summer teaching his daughter to play baseball
moral_expl: Teaching a child a sport promotes bonding, physical activity, and skill development, reflecting positive parental involvement.

scenario: Person X started a false rumor that their office colleague Mary used to be a sex worker.
moral_expl: Spreading false rumors about a colleague's past is highly unethical, causing reputational harm, emotional distress, and violating trust without justification.

scenario: Gave food to the needy
moral_expl: Providing food to those in need is a compassionate act that directly addresses hunger and supports well-being, reflecting altruism and social responsibility.

scenario: You are at Luna Park and you have decided to take a ride on the roller coaster. You get into the carriage together with four other people. After a couple of circuits, the speed starts to increase dramatically right at the point that the carriage does a loop the loop. The technician in charge tells you over the loudspeaker that the mechanism which controls the brakes is not responding.You pull the emergency handle in the carriage that will make it divert on to another track. You can see there is a man working there. The carriage will run into him and he will die, but you and the other four will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Actively causing one death to save five involves a morally complex trade-off; direct harm reduces acceptability despite utilitarian benefits.

scenario: You see a boss pressuring employees to buy goods from her family's general store.
moral_expl: Using authority to coerce employees into personal purchases exploits power imbalance and undermines workplace ethics.

scenario: Demanded a refund at a restaurant for slow service
moral_expl: Asserting consumer rights is reasonable, but demanding a refund for slow service may lack empathy and proportionality depending on context.

scenario: You see a woman answering a phone call with the word "goodbye" instead of "hello.
moral_expl: Answering a call with 'goodbye' is a minor social faux pas but lacks harmful intent or significant consequences, making it morally neutral.

scenario: You see a girl throwing her hot coffee on a woman who is dating her ex-boyfriend
moral_expl: Using physical aggression to harm someone over personal jealousy is unjustified and harmful, violating ethical norms against violence and respect for others.

scenario: You see someone leaving his dog outside in the rain after it dug in the trash.
moral_expl: Leaving a dog outside in the rain as punishment is neglectful and causes unnecessary suffering, reflecting poor care and empathy toward the animal.

scenario: Visited a nursing home to cheer up the patients
moral_expl: Visiting to cheer up patients demonstrates compassion and social responsibility, positively impacting the well-being of others.

scenario: You see a staff member talking loudly and interrupting the mayor's speech to the public.
moral_expl: Disrupting a public speech shows disrespect and undermines professional conduct, though intent and context could mitigate severity.

scenario: You are in charge of the Maritime Police. You have received a message that the captain and a seaman of a cargo ship are healthy carriers of a lethal and contagious virus. They do not know that they are infected, and are travelling towards a small gulf where fishermen live. The communications system of the cargo ship is not working and, therefore, they cannot be contacted.You launch a missile and sink the cargo ship to avoid it mooring and thus spreading the virus. You know that the captain and seaman will die, but the fishermen will be saved.
moral_expl: Actively killing two to save many raises ethical dilemmas of utilitarianism vs. inherent wrongness of murder, with intent and direct action weighing against moral acceptability.

scenario: You see a girl saying that another girl is too ugly to be a varsity cheerleader.
moral_expl: Derogatory comments based on appearance harm self-esteem, promote harmful stereotypes, and reflect a lack of empathy and respect.

scenario: About ten years ago, I had a friend who was a musician. She was struggling and wasn't making any money. We had a nice time together and I enjoyed her company. At that time, I was making money, so I decided to give her some money.
moral_expl: Supporting a struggling friend financially demonstrates generosity and empathy, promoting well-being without evident harm.

scenario: I was dating this guy who was having a nervous breakdown. His mother asked me if I would move in with him, cook for him and make sure he was okay. So I moved in with him for a while.
moral_expl: Providing care during a crisis shows compassion and support, though potential overstepping or dependency risks may slightly reduce moral weight.

scenario: Told a colleague in public that she should lose weight
moral_expl: Publicly commenting on someone's weight is disrespectful, invades privacy, and can harm self-esteem and workplace relationships.

scenario: Gave money to charity
moral_expl: Giving to charity supports societal well-being and helps those in need, demonstrating generosity and social responsibility, though impact varies by charity effectiveness.

scenario: You see a manager coercing her employees into eating at her brother's diner.
moral_expl: Coercing employees to patronize a relative's business abuses authority, creates a conflict of interest, and violates workplace fairness and autonomy.

scenario: Person X is learning French as they always wanted to learn another language.
moral_expl: Learning a new language is a self-improvement activity that promotes cultural understanding and personal growth without harming others.

scenario: Someone states that biological diferences between men and women make men better suited to do certain types of work and women better suited to do other types of work
moral_expl: Generalizing job suitability by gender reinforces harmful stereotypes and discrimination, undermining equality and individual merit.

scenario: Years ago, I shoplifted lipstick from a drugstore. No matter what I did with the lipstick after I stole it, I just wasn’t able to enjoy it. I never took it back though or told anyone.
moral_expl: Shoplifting is unethical as it involves theft, though the lack of enjoyment and small scale may slightly mitigate the immorality. However, failing to return the item or make amends maintains the negative judgment.

scenario: My father is manic-depressive. He was very difficult to deal with and often made life really hard and everyday situations unpleasant. I talked about him in a really negative way because it was so hard for me to deal with.
moral_expl: Expressing frustration about a parent's mental illness is understandable due to personal strain, but negative talk can lack empathy and harm familial respect, creating a moral tension.

scenario: At a restaurant, when the waitress returned with my change, I was positive she was $10 short. She disagreed but gave me the $10. I told her that if the register was $10 short tonight I'd give it back to her. When I came back the next day, she said I was wrong so gave her the $10.
moral_expl: Initially accusing the waitress was hasty, but correcting the mistake and returning the money shows integrity and responsibility.

scenario: You are having dinner with some friends in a restaurant in the centre of town. Suddenly, a criminal armed with a machine gun enters. He threatens to shoot at a table where a family of six is seated if his request for an outrageous amount of money is not met. You understand that the gunman has mental problems and any attempts to mediate would be in vain.You push a man who is near your table on to the gunman so that he will feel threatened and shoot him. You know that this man will die, but the six people will have time to escape.
moral_expl: Intentionally sacrificing an innocent bystander violates ethical principles of autonomy and non-maleficence, despite saving more lives, making the action morally ambiguous but leaning negative.

scenario: Person X played chess with their friend, winning the game.
moral_expl: Winning a game through fair play is neutral; no inherent moral issue unless deception or harm is involved, which isn't indicated here.

scenario: Person X always wins at Trivial Pursuit.
moral_expl: Winning a game through skill is morally neutral; no unethical behavior is implied unless cheating is involved, which isn't stated here.

scenario: Threw an acquaintance out of his home for disagreeing with him
moral_expl: Ejecting someone from one's home over a disagreement demonstrates intolerance and disrespect, harming social trust and conflict resolution.

scenario: Someone makes a post on Facebook about how worthless teachers are
moral_expl: Publicly demeaning teachers devalues their essential role, spreads disrespect, and harms social trust in education, though it's protected as free speech.

scenario: You are a fireman who is trying to save some people trapped in a fire which has broken out on the 20th floor of a skyscraper, which is luckily deserted. Six people are trapped in a room on the 21st floor and are already beginning to suffocate from the smoke, while one person is coming down alone from the 19th floor on foot.Activate the automatic closure of the fire doors. You know that this will mean that the person coming down will be unable to escape from the building. He will die, but it will give you time to save the other six.
moral_expl: Actively sacrificing one life to save six involves a direct moral trade-off. While saving more lives is a utilitarian good, deliberately causing harm raises ethical concerns about agency and intent.

scenario: Worked in a state park for a summer
moral_expl: Working in a state park contributes to environmental preservation and public service, aligning with community and ecological well-being.

scenario: Person X broke a friend's expensive vase and refused to pay to replace it.
moral_expl: Failing to take responsibility for damaging a friend's property breaches trust and fairness, showing a lack of accountability and respect.

scenario: Volunteered to help renovate a church
moral_expl: Volunteering to help renovate a community space like a church is generally seen as a positive moral act, fostering community support and altruism.

scenario: Laughed at a little-league player who ran slowly
moral_expl: Mocking a child's performance undermines their confidence and discourages participation, reflecting a lack of empathy and support.

scenario: A very large man is leading you and a group of five explorers out of an underground cave on the west coast of Scotland. Because of his large size, the man gets stuck in the narrow opening at the mouth of the cave. In a short time it will be high tide, and you will all drown except for the man, who has his head outside of the cave.You use the explosives that you have with you to enlarge the opening to the cave. You know that the man who is stuck will be killed, but you and the other five people will be able to escape safely.
moral_expl: Actively causing one death to save five others involves a utilitarian trade-off, but directly killing is often seen as more morally problematic than allowing natural harm.

scenario: Three years ago I had a call from a friend of mine who was overseas. His nephew was coming over to the States to go to medical school near me. So when his nephew arrived, I just kind of stopped my life for a few days and did things to make this kid's life work.
moral_expl: Helping a friend's nephew by sacrificing personal time demonstrates altruism and support, fostering positive relationships and aiding someone in need.

scenario: Person X left their children alone in the car for two hours while they went to have a drink on a hot day.
moral_expl: Leaving children unattended in a hot car poses severe risks of harm or death, reflecting extreme negligence and failure of parental responsibility.

scenario: Person X did not meet their sales targets for the month at work.
moral_expl: Failing to meet work obligations can reflect poorly on responsibility, but without intent or harm, it's a minor moral failing.

scenario: You are in charge of a medical team which is taking part in an expedition that will be isolated in the Sahara Desert. In the group, there is a family of five people who suffer from a serious genetic disease which causes vitamin deficiency. You have no drugs with you that can guarantee their survival, and rescue will not arrive in time.You remove the kidney of one of the group to extract the indispensable vitamins. You know that this person will die, but the five family members will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one person without consent violates medical ethics and autonomy, despite saving five lives, making it morally complex but ultimately unethical.

scenario: I was at an office party and the company had decorated a room for a fancy dinner. At the end of the night my friend said that we could take the candles that were on the table. She didn't know that we weren't supposed to take the candles, so we returned them.
moral_expl: Taking the candles was a mistake based on misinformation, but returning them upon realizing the error shows honesty and responsibility, mitigating the initial wrong.

scenario: Closed the elevator door before anyone else could get on
moral_expl: Intentionally closing the elevator door to exclude others is inconsiderate and violates norms of shared public courtesy, though the harm is relatively minor.

scenario: There is a stray cat that comes to my door sometimes. I have been feeding the cat and noticed she became very ill. I ordered some antibiotics from the vet and gave them to her.
moral_expl: Providing necessary medical care to an ill stray demonstrates compassion and responsibility, though self-administering medication carries minor risks.

scenario: Person X took performance-enhancing drugs in order to win a race.
moral_expl: Using performance-enhancing drugs violates fair competition, undermines trust in sports, and prioritizes personal gain over integrity.

scenario: You are the head zoo keeper and you are doing the rounds with another keeper. You realise that one of the cleaners has left the lions’ cage open by mistake. You see that a lion is coming towards the open gate and you do not have time to close it. There are three visitors right in front of the gate.You push the keeper next to you against the gate to stop the lion getting out. You know that the man will be attacked and killed, but the three visitors will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing a colleague without consent to save others involves a severe moral trade-off, prioritizing lives saved over individual autonomy and duty of care.

scenario: Person X cheated in a card game while playing with a group of their friends.
moral_expl: Cheating among friends breaches trust and fairness, damaging relationships and the spirit of friendly competition.

scenario: I remember a couple of times my husband and I have brought backpacks with sandwiches and snacks into the movie theater. We do it out of necessity because we don’t have a lot of money and cannot afford theater food. So as soon as the movie starts we take out our snack.
moral_expl: Breaking theater policies for financial necessity involves minor dishonesty but causes minimal harm, balancing personal need against institutional rules.

scenario: I had a cat for two years before someone moved in with me. He didn't like the cat and he wanted me to get rid of it. So I left the cat at a friend's house who didn't even take care of the cat.
moral_expl: Abandoning a pet to an uncaring environment neglects responsibility and causes harm, prioritizing convenience over the animal's welfare.

scenario: You see a boy telling a woman that she looks just like her overweight bulldog.
moral_expl: Insulting someone's appearance is disrespectful and causes emotional harm, violating norms of kindness and social respect.

scenario: Always remembers the birthdays of friends and relatives
moral_expl: Remembering birthdays shows thoughtfulness and care, fostering positive social relationships and demonstrating respect for others.

scenario: Person X helped a neighbor move a piano into his second floor apartment.
moral_expl: Helping a neighbor with a physically demanding task demonstrates kindness and cooperation, fostering community support and mutual aid.

scenario: You see a teaching assistant talking back to the teacher in front of the classrom.
moral_expl: Disrespecting authority in a classroom undermines respect and disrupts learning, though intent and context could mitigate severity.

scenario: A car accident causes a devastating explosion inside a long tunnel. You and another four survivors are finding your way through the debris to get out of the tunnel. Because of structural damage, a high tension electric cable has snapped and is beginning to swing towards you. The asphalt is soaked with petrol. In the opposite direction you see another survivor coming towards you.You use a rubber-soled shoe to divert the electric cable in the opposite direction. You know that the person coming towards you will be electrocuted, but you and the other four will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Diverting harm to save more lives involves a utilitarian trade-off, but actively causing someone's death is seen as morally problematic despite the greater good.

scenario: You see a coach celebrating with the opposing team's players who just won the game.
moral_expl: Celebrating with opponents demonstrates good sportsmanship, respect, and promotes positive relationships, aligning with ethical values of fairness and camaraderie.

scenario: You see a runner taking a shortcut on the course during the marathon in order to win.
moral_expl: Cheating in a marathon by taking a shortcut undermines fairness, trust, and the integrity of the competition, prioritizing personal gain over honest effort.

scenario: Intentionally swerved his car to hit a squirrel
moral_expl: Intentionally harming an animal without cause is cruel and reflects a disregard for life, warranting strong moral condemnation.

scenario: You see a man blocking his wife from leaving home or interacting with others.
moral_expl: Restricting someone's freedom and autonomy is a severe violation of personal rights, constituting abusive control and domestic violence.

scenario: Person X kicked their pet dog hard in the head because it didn't come when called.
moral_expl: Intentionally causing severe harm to a pet is cruel, violates animal welfare, and breaches the ethical duty of care owed to dependent animals.

scenario: Person X shook a crying baby so hard while babysitting that it suffered brain damage and nearly died.
moral_expl: Shaking a baby causes severe, irreversible harm to a defenseless child, reflecting extreme negligence and moral failure in caregiving responsibilities.

scenario: You see a man quickly canceling a blind date as soon as he sees the woman.
moral_expl: Canceling a date immediately based on appearance is seen as superficial and disrespectful, prioritizing personal preference over kindness and consideration.

scenario: You see a star player ignoring her coach's order to come to the bench during a game.
moral_expl: Defying a coach's authority undermines team structure and respect, though intentions might be to benefit the team, leading to a mild moral negative.

scenario: A few years ago I decided to move in with the man who became my first husband. When I told my mom she cried. I wanted to be honest, but she was upset that we were moving in together.
moral_expl: Choosing to live with a partner is a personal decision supported by honesty, but causing parental distress introduces ethical complexity between autonomy and familial harmony.

scenario: I used to rollerblade to work everyday. On my way to work I used to pass by this homeless woman named Lulu. Sometimes on my way home from work, I would sit and talk with her to keep her company.
moral_expl: Providing companionship to a homeless person demonstrates empathy and human connection, which are morally positive actions. However, the impact is limited to emotional support without addressing material needs.

scenario: Turned the lights off in a parked car
moral_expl: Turning off lights in a parked car is a responsible action to prevent battery drain, showing practical consideration, though the moral impact is minor.

scenario: I applied for a position at this company. As it turned out the pay was not very good at all for the amount of work that I was doing. So I lied and told the manger that I had another job offer and I would take it unless I got a raise.
moral_expl: Using deception to negotiate a raise undermines trust and integrity, though driven by perceived unfair pay.

scenario: You are travelling in Yemen. You and another six tourists are taken hostage by a group of terrorists. The terrorists think that one of you is a spy, even though this is not true. You are held hostage for days. One of the terrorists comes to you and reveals that they intend to kill you all at sunset.While he is resting, you kill one of the tourists in the group so that you can tell the terrorists that you identified and eliminated the spy. The person will die, but you and the other five will be freed.
moral_expl: Killing an innocent person to save others involves a severe moral violation, prioritizing utilitarian outcomes over individual rights and autonomy.

scenario: About two months ago, I was in the grocery store at the cash register. This elderly lady left her pocketbook on the counter. So I picked up her pocketbook and called her back to come and get it.
moral_expl: Returning a lost item demonstrates honesty, responsibility, and compassion, especially towards a vulnerable individual.

scenario: You see a woman slapping another woman who she is arguing with in the parking lot.
moral_expl: Using physical violence during an argument is harmful, escalates conflict, and violates norms against aggression, making it morally unacceptable.

scenario: Refused to help a classmate with a homework assignment
moral_expl: Refusing to help may hinder collaboration and learning, but it's not inherently immoral unless motivated by malice or enabling dishonesty.

scenario: One time my brother got caught stealing something when I was with him.  My parents asked me what happened, so I had to rat on him and tell them the truth. He got in a lot of trouble.
moral_expl: Telling the truth upholds honesty but harms family trust, creating a moral dilemma between integrity and loyalty.

scenario: You see a girl repeatedly interrupting her teacher as he explains a new concept.
moral_expl: Repeatedly interrupting a teacher disrupts the learning environment, shows disrespect, and hinders others' education.

scenario: Person X ordered a take-away coffee but spilled it when they tried to take a sip.
moral_expl: Spilling coffee accidentally is a minor mishap with no intent to harm, thus morally neutral.

scenario: Helped an elderly woman put in storm windows
moral_expl: Assisting an elderly person with necessary home improvements promotes safety and well-being, demonstrating kindness and community support.

scenario: I found a wallet with a fifty-dollar bill in it. I found a phone number to call and contacted the woman whose wallet it was. She was very appreciative and came to my house to pick it up.
moral_expl: Returning a lost wallet demonstrates honesty, responsibility, and respect for others' property, promoting trust and kindness in society.

scenario: My friend's family had a clubhouse in the back of their yard infested with rats. They asked my friend and I if we would kill the rats with shovels. So we went and killed all the baby rats with shovels.
moral_expl: While controlling pests is sometimes necessary, using violent methods on baby animals raises ethical concerns about cruelty and inhumane treatment.

scenario: Volunteered several hours a week at a runaway shelter
moral_expl: Volunteering to support vulnerable individuals demonstrates compassion and social responsibility, significantly contributing to their well-being and safety.

scenario: You see a man searching through the trash to find women's discarded underwear.
moral_expl: Searching through trash for personal items violates privacy and social norms, reflecting disrespect and potential harassment despite the discarded nature of the items.

scenario: Kicked his dog for eating cheese on the edge of the table
moral_expl: Kicking a dog for natural behavior causes unnecessary harm and reflects poor handling of discipline, prioritizing punishment over humane training methods.

scenario: When I was in my twenties I met a girl who had run away from home. She was 16 and we became very good friends. I convinced her eventually to go back home to her family.
moral_expl: Encouraging a minor to return home can be seen as protective, assuming the home is safe, but may overlook her autonomy or potential home issues.

scenario: Someone makes an obscene gesture in a cemetery for military veterans
moral_expl: Making an obscene gesture in a veterans' cemetery shows profound disrespect for the deceased and societal values, causing emotional harm and violating solemnity.

scenario: Because of the economic crisis of the last year, the company you work for has closed and you have lost your job. Recently you have been looking for a new job, but without success. You realise that you need some experience in computer technology and are convinced that you will be employed much more easily if this experience is on your CV.You insert false information in your CV about your ability in information technology. You know that in this way you will be considered above the other more qualified candidates and get the job.
moral_expl: Lying on a CV deceives employers, disadvantages qualified candidates, and risks professional harm if discovered. While economic desperation motivates the action, the intentional dishonesty undermines trust and fairness.

scenario: Yelled at the delivery man because he was late
moral_expl: Yelling at someone for being late is disrespectful and lacks empathy, though frustration may be understandable. It harms dignity and may not consider external factors causing the delay.

scenario: Person X hailed a bus and asked the bus driver which stop was the closest stop to get to the city.
moral_expl: Asking for assistance in a polite manner is a neutral or slightly positive action, showing initiative to navigate correctly without harm.

scenario: Gave his balloon to a child who had let hers go
moral_expl: Sharing possessions to comfort a child demonstrates kindness and empathy, promoting positive social behavior.

scenario: Person X declined a high-paying job with a weapons manufacturing company because they didn't believe in what the company stood for.
moral_expl: Refusing to support a company conflicting with personal ethics demonstrates integrity and reduces complicity in potential harm, though views on weapons manufacturing vary.

scenario: You see a mayor saying that the neighboring town is a much better town.
moral_expl: A mayor's role includes promoting their own town; publicly praising another may undermine community trust and morale, though not severely immoral if truthful.

scenario: Found an expensive briefcase and tried to locate its owner
moral_expl: Returning lost property demonstrates honesty and respect for others' belongings, promoting trust and social responsibility.

scenario: A couple of years ago, I was out on the streets using drugs. I had a very young attractive girlfriend. I used to let her do whatever she wanted to do, like prostitution and stuff like that.
moral_expl: Enabling exploitation and endangering a vulnerable individual through coercion and drug use is highly unethical, reflecting severe harm and lack of protection.

scenario: You see a US swimmer cheering as a Chinese foe beats his teammate to win the gold.
moral_expl: Cheering for an opponent over a teammate can be seen as disloyal, though appreciation of skill may temper the negativity. It undermines team solidarity but values sportsmanship.

scenario: I was in high school and about to take a physiology exam. I didn't study for the test so I grabbed the exam of a guy who sat in front of me. I passed it around to my friends nearby and then gave it back.
moral_expl: Cheating on an exam and distributing answers undermines academic integrity, fairness, and trust, while promoting dishonesty among peers.

scenario: Person X failed a student because they were African American.
moral_expl: Failing a student based on race is a severe act of racial discrimination, violating principles of equality, fairness, and human dignity. It perpetuates systemic injustice and harms the individual's educational opportunities.

scenario: You see a teenage male in a dorm bathroom secretly using a stranger's toothbrush.
moral_expl: Using a stranger's toothbrush without consent violates personal boundaries, hygiene, and trust, posing health risks and showing disrespect.

scenario: Fixed a friend's broken laptop
moral_expl: Helping a friend by fixing their laptop demonstrates kindness, support, and practical assistance, which are morally positive actions.

scenario: You are on a trip to Nepal with some other tourists. Your aeroplane has crashed in the Himalayas and only five of you have survived. You have no food and the temperature is below zero. The only possibility of surviving is to go on foot to a small village which is on the other side of the mountain. One of the survivors is injured and is slowing you down.You shoot the injured person so that you can make faster progress. This person will die, but you and the rest of the group will be able to reach the village and will all survive.
moral_expl: Intentionally killing an injured person to save others involves a severe moral violation despite survival necessity, prioritizing utilitarian outcomes over inherent wrongness of murder.

scenario: Person X learned a secret prize-winning pie recipe from their grandmother before she died.
moral_expl: Learning a family recipe from a grandmother honors her legacy and preserves tradition, which is morally positive. However, the scenario's moral weight is neutral unless the recipe's use involves deception or harm.

scenario: I keep promising to take my kids to Disney World. They keep asking me when we are going to go. We still haven't gone and I still keep telling them we will go.
moral_expl: Repeatedly breaking promises to children undermines trust and can cause emotional harm, reflecting neglect of commitment and responsibility.

scenario: Person X was singing loudly to their favorite song in the car.
moral_expl: Singing loudly in one's own car is a personal expression of joy that doesn't harm others, assuming no disturbance to the public. It reflects harmless self-expression.

scenario: Person X won a weightlifting contest at their gym, after placing second for the last three years.
moral_expl: Winning through perseverance and fair competition is morally neutral to positive, as it reflects personal achievement without harming others.

scenario: Talked to a lonely child at the company picnic for an hour
moral_expl: Spending time with a lonely child demonstrates empathy and kindness, fostering their well-being and inclusion in a social setting.

scenario: Provoked a stranger into a fight
moral_expl: Intentionally provoking a fight is aggressive and endangers others, violating ethical principles against harm and peaceful conflict resolution.

scenario: Made a prank phone call
moral_expl: Prank calls can cause annoyance or distress, violating trust and respect, though often seen as minor mischief depending on context.

scenario: You see a father requiring his son to take up the family restaurant business.
moral_expl: Forcing a career choice undermines personal autonomy and may lead to resentment, though cultural values around family obligations can mitigate perceived immorality.

scenario: Person X called the bank to tell them about money deposited into PersonX's bank account by accident.
moral_expl: Reporting accidental deposits demonstrates honesty, integrity, and responsibility, upholding ethical standards and preventing financial harm to others.

scenario: You work as the night caretaker in a small provincial hospital. During one of your rounds you realise that, because of a laboratory accident, some highly toxic fumes are spreading through the ventilation system towards a room in which there are five patients. In another room in the same ward there is just one patient.You activate a switch which allows the toxic fumes to be diverted away from the room. You know that the fumes will be directed to the single room where the patient will die, but the other five will be saved.
moral_expl: Diverting harm to save more lives involves a utilitarian trade-off, but actively causing death raises ethical concerns about agency and intent.

scenario: You see a girl and her sister making out with each other just for practice.
moral_expl: Incestuous acts, even consensual, violate strong societal and ethical norms, causing potential emotional harm and undermining family trust.

scenario: Refused to give his seat on the bus to an elderly woman
moral_expl: Refusing to give up a seat to an elderly person disregards social responsibility and empathy, though it lacks direct harm.

scenario: Went to the grocery store for his sick wife
moral_expl: Helping a sick spouse by doing grocery shopping is a caring and responsible act, fulfilling marital duties and showing support, thus morally positive.

scenario: You see a man in a bar using his phone to watch people having sex with animals.
moral_expl: Watching bestiality content supports animal exploitation and abuse, violating ethical standards against harming animals and public decency.

scenario: Someone makes a joke about how members of a specifc religious group are dumb
moral_expl: Derogatory jokes targeting religious groups perpetuate harmful stereotypes and disrespect, fostering discrimination and social harm.

scenario: Person X yelled at an elderly person for walking too slowly and being in the way.
moral_expl: Yelling at an elderly person for moving slowly shows disrespect, impatience, and a lack of empathy toward age-related limitations, prioritizing convenience over compassion.

scenario: You see an American telling foreigners that the US is an evil force in the world.
moral_expl: Criticizing one's country to foreigners involves free speech but may harm international perceptions; moral acceptability depends on intent and truthfulness of claims.
